title: Mettl Secure Browser Test Taker Guide
text:
![]()

## System check and mock test

Mercer | Mettl is an online assessment examination platform which allows you to take a test in a secure environment from any location. You only need a live internet connection and a laptop/desktop to get you started.

[]()

### System Requirements

* **Network Speed -** Minimum 512 kbps - Click here to check your network speed
 
* **Supported Browsers -** Chrome version 63 and above\* (Windows/Mac), Firefox version 52 and above (Windows/Linux/Mac), Safari Firefox version 12 and above
 
* **Browser Settings -** The Mettl test window may fail to open due to your browser's pop-up blocker setting. To avoid this, please turn off the pop-up blocker or permit pop-ups for tests.mettl.com in your browser configuration.
 
* **Firewall Settings -** Certain local network restrictions in the network firewall may be preventing the launch of Mettl tests. If you face any such issues, please get in touch with your local network administrator and share these guidelines with them.
 

\* (32.0.1700.76 is not supported)

### Please note

Some assessments may not be supported on mobile devices; hence, it is recommended that you take all tests on a desktop or laptop.

### Follow these simple steps to give the test

### Timeline

Download the Mercer | Mettl Secure Browser from the link below and follow the steps in the video.

Please ensure you have attempted the mock test using the above link. Once you have completed the mock test, proceed to the next step.

Please ensure you have attempted the mock test using the above link.

You can access the actual test using:

* Press the start test button on your invitation email.
 
* Or paste the test link/URL shared with you in a browser and hit enter.
 
* Or enter the test invitation key on tests.mettl.com
 

Once you have reached the test page, click on the "Launch Test" button and follow the steps in the video given.

![]()

## What is the Mercer | Mettl Secure Browser (MSB)?

The Mercer | Mettl Secure Browser (MSB) is a web-based lockdown browser that converts any system into a safe workstation. 
 
MSB ensures that test-takers cannot exit the application before the test is completed. It also prevents them from opening unauthorized websites or accessing blocked software or third-party applications. 
 
MSB also shuts down all active applications, such as other browsers, applications, and Windows. It restricts access to other activities, such as switching to other websites and screens during the exam.

## Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)

If your camera/mic permissions are cancelled, check your browser-level permissions. Also, check for hardware issues with the camera/microphone if the problem remains unresolved. You can also visit onlinemictest.com to check the functionality of the camera and microphone. Furthermore, check your privacy settings. If the problem persists, switch to another browser to start the test, and if the problem is not resolved, please switch the system.

The test might get submitted due to the following reasons:

* The candidate clicked on the Finish Test button.
 
* Disconnection limit exceeded.
 
* The candidate closed the test window.
 
* Test duration is exhausted.
 

This message appears when the candidate cannot access the test link or the time window to attempt the test is over.

To attempt the test, follow the given steps:

* Access/launch your test using either of the following methods (depending upon the approach adopted by your test administrator).
 

* Press the start test button on your invitation email.
 
* Or paste the test link/URL shared with you in a browser and hit enter.
 
* Or enter the test invitation key on tests.mettl.com
 

* Check for system compatibility.
 
* Allow access to camera/microphone.
 
* Allow permissions for screen sharing.
 
* Fill in the candidate registration field.
 
* Allow for face capture.
 
* Present your ID card for verification.
 
* Proceed to attempt the test.
 

You might get disconnected if the internet connection is unstable and drops below 512 kbps. Please check your network connection and ensure that it is stable.

### Featured Banner

## The Mercer | Mettl platform is GDPR-ready.

### If you have any concerns, please contact us at 
mettl-support@mercer.com.

## Things to remember

1.

#### Please make sure you download the latest version of MSB from the test link given to you.

2.

#### The operating software on your laptop should be up to date.

3.

#### Please test your camera & microphone before you start the test.

4.

#### Taking assessments with MSB

MSB is a web-based lockdown browser that converts any system into a safe workstation.

* It does not allow test-takers to leave the application before finishing the test. It also restricts candidates from opening unauthorized websites or accessing blocked software/third-party applications.
 
* MSB also shuts down all active applications, such as other browsers and cheating tools. It restricts access to different activities, such as switching to other websites and screens during the exam.
 
* Mettl Secure Browser (MSB) doesn't have any navigation buttons, but the optional reload button in the taskbar or the key F5 can be used to reload the current page \*(avoid pressing F5 key as it also submits the test if pressed while attempting the questions)
 
* Screen sharing using cable: If a candidate's screen is shared before starting MSB, It will only let the candidate begin the test once they remove the screen-sharing cable. The test will terminate if the candidate shares a screen after starting MSB.
 

5.

#### Browser cache settings:

It is always advisable to clear browser cache/cookies before starting Mercer | Mettl tests. For most browsers, pressing "CTRL+SHIFT+DELETE" and selecting "Clear Cache" (for Firefox and Chrome) does the job. If any candidate faces issues loading the test or images, shut down the system directly using the power button and then clear the cache again.0

6.

#### System updates:

Mercer | Mettl also recommends turning off security patches, updates of the anti-virus definitions and application updates (manually) during the test to avoid any interference while taking the test. You can turn off automatic system updates for the test duration as they may lead to heavy downloads during the test, and auto-system restart will hamper your test progress. To disable System Auto-updates, follow the below steps: 

* To disable System Auto-updates, follow the below steps: Go to 'Start' -> 'Run' -> Type Services for 'Windows.msc' and then stop the 'Automatic Updates' service.
 
* If you get a prompt that 'Ports 1987 and 1988 have been blocked', please contact your network administrator to assist you with the system permissions needed to run a web-cam proctored test.
